turns.content: Hey, quick check — how many marks is the Fault analysis section worth again? I want to make sure I'm balancing my study time right.
The Fault analysis section is worth 15 marks. It's definitely a good area to focus on since it's high-yield.
Got it. For that section, can I get partial credit if I identify thin evidence but don’t fully explain it?
Yes, you can receive partial credit for identifying aspects of thin evidence even if your explanation isn't complete. Just make sure to link it to your claims when possible.
Cool! I remember you mentioning that I should avoid generic terms in my answers. Can you remind me why that is?
Using specific terms helps clarify your reasoning and supports your claims better. Generic terms can make your arguments vague, which is less convincing in your answers.
Right, that makes sense. I was also thinking about the BFT topic again — how do I make sure I remember that it’s 3f+1 nodes needed?
You could create a mnemonic or an acronym to help you remember the 3f+1 formula. Also, practice applying it in different scenarios to reinforce your memory.
Yeah, I’ll try that! By the way, is vector clocks something worth studying, or should I just skip it?
Vector clocks are actually frequently tested, so I’d recommend at least a brief review. Even though they seem minor, they could help you secure those easy marks.
Hmm, I’m still not convinced they’re worth my time. I mean, the past exam had only one question on it!
True, but remember that even one question can be a significant part of your score. Plus, understanding it might help you in related topics on the exam.
Fair point! I’ll give it a quick look. Can you remind me how many marks the Consensus section is worth?
The Consensus section is worth 15 marks, just like the Fault analysis section. It's another high-yield area to focus on.
Awesome! Last thing — what's the most common mistake students make in the Trace reasoning section?
The most common mistake is narrative confusion, where students mix up the order of events. Make sure to keep the sender-first chronology clear in your answers.
Thanks for the reminder! I’ll make sure to watch out for that. Time to hit the books!
Good luck with your studying! Remember to take breaks and stay focused on those high-yield topics. You've got this!
